figures.caption: Table 4: Experimental predictions and corresponding theoretical results (Experiments 1–3 and 5). Experiment 4 results are tabulated in the caption of Figure 2.

Figure 3: Experiment 5 (end-to-end, SST-2, d = 64, θw = 0.8, λreg = 0.05, 10 epochs). Left: training loss and validation accuracy over 10 epochs; final validation accuracy 69.4%. Centre: residual content λmax(Ares) at the five growth events, strictly decreasing (4.36→ 2.38→ 2.15→ 1.35→ 0.84), confirming the spectral ordering prediction. Right: separation force F(h) sep (bars) and prototype spread S(P(h)) (green curve) per head; S(P(h)) > 0 for all heads confirms assumption (A1); Fsep is partially ordered, with deviations attributable to end-to-end co-adaptation after growth events.

Figure 2: Experiment 4 (Colab, BERT/SST-2, frozen encoder, 3 epochs). (a) Directional energy Γh per head: values range from 0.15 to 0.32, confirming head differentiation on real data. (b) max |S(P(h′)) after − S(P(h′)) before| = 0 at all six pruning events (log scale; bars touch 10−15), verifying Theorem 6(i) with machine precision. (c) Actual Fsep drop (blue) equals Fsep of the pruned head (red) exactly at every event (all labelled OK), verifying Lemma 14: prototype subspace orthogonality makes the drop decompose exactly.
Table 1: Origin of the main results. Inherited: used without modification from cited work. Extended: strengthened or generalised here. New: proved for the first time in this paper.
Table 2: Complete list of assumptions. (H1)–(H5) inherited from [3].
